##NAMES=Keyite
##RRUFFID=R050585
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=([box]_0.5_Cu_0.5_)CuCdZn_2_(AsO_4_)_3_·H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Tsumeb mine, Tsumeb, Otavi District, Oshikoto, Namibia
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=University of Arizona Mineral Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Microscopic blue prismatic crystals embedded in green zincolivenite, R050583, and associated with schultenite, R050584
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R050585
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Cu^2+^_2.67_Fe_0.33_)Zn_4.00_(Cd_1.65_Fe_0.14_Pb_0.11_Mn_0.06_Ca_0.04_)(As_1.00_O_4_)_6_·2H_2_O ; = lighter phase. The darker phase: Zn_1.00_(Cu_0.91_Fe_0.09_)(As_1.00_O_4_)(OH)  is zincolivenite
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.74 b: 12.92 c: 6.89 alpha: 90 beta: 99.3 gamma: 90 volume: 1032 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
